properties.WarningTitle: AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN) - St George - fire as at  3:28pm Wednesday,  6 May 2026
properties.WarningText:
AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN) - St George - fire as at  3:28pm Wednesday,  6 May 2026 

 

Warning level: ADVICE

 


Warning area: St George and surrounding areas


 

Smoke is currently affecting St George and surrounding areas. This is from a hazard reduction burn near Salmon Road and the Carnarvon Highway. This fire is under control.

 

What you should do:

- Stay inside and close windows and doors.

- Keep respiratory medication close by.

- Avoid driving through smoke if you can.

- Watch out for firefighters working in the area.

- Do not fly drones around the fire. Drones affect air operations.



 

Impacts:

- Smoke can make it hard for some people to breathe.

- Smoke can make it hard to see when you are driving.

- No properties are at risk.



 

Further Information:

- For all warnings, visit the QFD Current Bushfires and Warnings website.

- Follow QFD on Facebook and X.com.

- For road closures, visit the QLD Traffic website or call 13 19 40.

- Visit the Department of Environment and Science website for live air quality data.

- Find out how to get ready for a bushfire on the QFD website under Prepare - Bushfire.



 

Updates will be issued if needed.

 

This warning is from Queensland Fire Department.

- Keep respiratory medication close by.

properties.WarningTitle: AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN) - Beerwah to Maroochydore - fire as at  1:55pm Thursday,  7 May 2026
properties.WarningText:
AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN) - Beerwah to Maroochydore - fire as at  1:55pm Thursday,  7 May 2026 

 

Warning level: ADVICE

 


Warning area: Beerwah, Caloundra, Buderim, Maroochydore and surrounding areas


 

Smoke is currently affecting Beerwah, Caloundra, Buderim, Maroochydore and surrounding areas. This is from a hazard reduction burn in the Beerwah Forest Reserve. This fire is under control.

 

What you should do:

- Stay inside and close windows and doors.

- Keep respiratory medication close by.

- Avoid driving through smoke if you can.

- Watch out for firefighters working in the area.

- Do not fly drones around the fire. Drones affect air operations.



 

Impacts:

- Smoke can make it hard for some people to breathe.

- Smoke can make it hard to see when you are driving.

- No properties are at risk.



 

Further Information:

- For all warnings, visit the QFD Current Bushfires and Warnings website.

- Follow QFD on Facebook and X.com.

- For road closures, visit the QLD Traffic website or call 13 19 40.

- Visit the Department of Environment and Science website for live air quality data.

- Find out how to get ready for a bushfire on the QFD website under Prepare - Bushfire.



 

Updates will be issued if needed.

 

This warning is from Queensland Fire Department.

properties.CallToAction: There is a vegetation fire at this location. Smoke may affect residents and motorists in the area. Residents should close their doors and windows and keep medication close by if suffering from a respiratory condition. Motorists should use caution and drive to conditions. If you believe your property is under threat, you should call Triple Zero (000) immediately.
